patches.summary: Added Chrome cookie import, local order history, cart planning, checkout, analytics, config defaults, and agent-context enrichment.
patches.reason: The generated OrderToGo CLI needed the requested agent-safe ordering workflow without re-running the Printing Press generator.
patches.validated_outcome: Build, vet, required command smoke checks, verify fixture, config dry-run, and validate-narrative passed with a workspace-local GOCACHE.

patches.summary: Preserve full modifier and sub-item trees when a menu record directly matches --search.
patches.reason: Direct menu matches should be returned intact; pruning their children in place can silently strip modifier data from matching items.

patches.summary: Three fixes for a fully-broken published CLI: implement real Chrome cookie decryption on macOS, spoof browser-like UA + Origin + Referer on every API call, and rewrite orders place to use the real POST /m/api/postmicmeshorder endpoint with Stripe saved-card auth.
patches.reason: As-published the CLI failed at three layers. (1) third_party/kooky/browser/chrome/chrome.go read encrypted_value as raw bytes and never decrypted them, so auth login --chrome silently imported zero cookies on macOS. (2) Even with valid cookies, ordertogo.com returned 403 Access denied for every /m/api/* call because the server gates on browser-like User-Agent + Origin + Referer headers and the CLI sent ordertogo-pp-cli/0.1.0 with no Origin. (3) The generated orders place command drove a headless-Chrome Braintree DropIn flow that the live site does not use; the real checkout endpoint is /m/api/postmicmeshorder and the payment processor is Stripe with a saved customer + card key.
patches.validated_outcome: Build and vet pass. auth login --chrome now imports 8 cookies on macOS (verified end-to-end against a live ordertogo.com session). Live /m/api/* requests return real responses (menu fetch, restaurant detail) instead of 403. PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY=1 dry-run of orders place produces a request body byte-for-byte identical to the postmicmeshorder body captured from a real successful $2.99 checkout in the browser.

patches.summary: Switch orders list from /m/api/getmicmeshorders (web SHOW path) to /api/getUserOrderHistoryByUserid (real LIST endpoint). Inject Authorization (from _fbtoken cookie) and __requestid headers globally. Resolve and cache meshuser id via /api/getRestmeshUser.
patches.reason: As shipped, `orders list` posted to /m/api/getmicmeshorders with empty body and got 400 'Server communication config missing'. That endpoint is the per-orderid SHOW path; the actual LIST endpoint /api/getUserOrderHistoryByUserid was missed during sniff. All /api/* calls also require Authorization=<_fbtoken JWT> (the web client lifts the cookie value into the header in fbbase.post) plus an __requestid idempotency header on POSTs. Without those headers every call returns the same 'Server communication config missing'. Fix: client.go injects both headers automatically; orders_list.go and sync.go's orders branch do a two-call flow (getRestmeshUser to obtain meshuser.id, then getUserOrderHistoryByUserid) and cache the id on Config.MeshUserID after the first lookup.
patches.validated_outcome: ordertogo-pp-cli orders list returns 58 real orders (Sep 2025 through May 2026). sync --orders ingests 58 rows. Verified against the same user account whose live /history page renders the same 58 entries from /api/getUserOrderHistoryByUserid.
